subject: ap-calculus-bc
title: Rate & Accumulation (Riemann Sums)
stimulus:
The temperature of water in a tub at time t is modeled by a strictly increasing, twice-differentiable function W,
where W(cid:11)t(cid:12) is measured in degrees Fahrenheit and t is measured in minutes. At time t (cid:32) 0, the temperature of
the water is 55(cid:146)F. The water is heated for 30 minutes, beginning at time t (cid:32) 0. Values of W(cid:11)t(cid:12) at selected
times t for the first 20 minutes are given in the table above.

parts.prompt:
Use the data in the table to estimate W(cid:132)(cid:11)12(cid:12). Show the computations that lead to your answer. Using correct
units, interpret the meaning of your answer in the context of this problem.
20

scoring_points.description: Correct estimate using difference quotient with supporting work

required_elements: Difference quotient using adjacent table values
The temperature of the water is increasing at approximately 1.017 °F/min at t = 12

parts.prompt:
Use the data in the table to evaluate (cid:212) W(cid:132)(cid:11)t(cid:12) dt. Using correct units, interpret the meaning of (cid:212) W(cid:132)(cid:11)t(cid:12)dt
0 0
in the context of this problem.
1

scoring_points.description: Correct value $\int_0^{20} W'(t)\, dt = W(20) - W(0) = 16$ °F by FTC

alternatives.correct_example: $\int_0^{20} W'(t)\, dt = W(20) - W(0) = 71.0 - 55.0 = 16$ degrees Fahrenheit

required_elements: The temperature of the water increased by 16 °F over the 20-minute period

common_traps: Stating the temperature is 16 °F rather than 'increased by' 16 °F

parts.prompt:
For 0 (cid:133) t (cid:133) 20, the average temperature of the water in the tub is (cid:212) W(cid:11)t(cid:12)dt. Use a left Riemann sum
20 0
1 20
with the four subintervals indicated by the data in the table to approximate (cid:212) W(cid:11)t(cid:12)dt. Does this
20 0
approximation overestimate or underestimate the average temperature of the water over these 20 minutes?
Explain your reasoning.

scoring_points.description: See College Board scoring guidelines for part (c), point 1
See College Board scoring guidelines for part (c), point 2
See College Board scoring guidelines for part (c), point 3

parts.prompt:
For 20 (cid:133) t (cid:133) 25, the function W that models the water temperature has first derivative given by
W(cid:132)(cid:11)t(cid:12) (cid:32) 0.4 tcos(cid:11)0.06t(cid:12). Based on the model, what is the temperature of the water at time t (cid:32) 25?
